name: Arizona Pharmacy Technician License
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Arizona Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.

address.description: 1700 W. Washington Street, Suite 250, Phoenix, AZ 85007

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Arizona pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Arizona State Board of Pharmacy lists an $82 pharmacy technician application fee and a $72 renewal fee.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Arizona have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Arizona?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Arizona State Board of Pharmacy does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing page. Use the board's application status tools to check progress.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Arizona Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Arizona.
